questions.stem: An application generates images with a gpt-image model and needs to display the result. In what form does the API return the generated image?

options.text: A temporary public URL that expires after 24 hours
Base64-encoded data in the b64_json field
A binary PNG stream with no wrapper
A link to Azure Blob Storage created automatically

questions.explanation: The gpt-image family always returns base64-encoded image data in the b64_json field rather than a hosted URL, so the client decodes the string to obtain the image bytes.

questions.stem: A developer integrates the Sora video generation model. Which interaction pattern is correct?

options.text: A single synchronous call that returns the finished video
Submit a job, poll for completion, then download the result
A streaming WebSocket that emits frames in real time
Upload frames and receive a stitched video immediately

questions.explanation: Video generation is a long-running operation, so the workflow submits a job, polls the job status until it completes, and then downloads the generated video. There is no synchronous single-call response.

questions.stem: A team wants to repaint only a specific region of an existing image while leaving the rest unchanged, using the image edits endpoint. What must the mask satisfy?

options.text: The mask must be a different size to indicate scaling
The mask must match the source image dimensions, with transparent pixels marking the repaint area
The mask must be provided as base64 JSON, not multipart
The mask must cover the whole image with a solid color

questions.explanation: The edits endpoint uses multipart/form-data and a mask whose dimensions match the source image. Transparent pixels in the mask mark the region to repaint while opaque pixels are preserved.

questions.stem: When submitting a Sora generation job, which parameter most directly drives billing?

options.text: n_variants, the number of candidate clips
n_seconds, the duration of the generated video
The width value only
The output filename

questions.explanation: Video generation is billed primarily by duration, so n_seconds is the main cost driver. Width and height are integer dimensions and n_variants controls how many clips are produced, but duration is the principal billing factor.

questions.stem: Which statements about controlling gpt-image generation are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: quality can be set to low, medium, or high
A transparent background is supported with the png output format on gpt-image-1
output_compression applies to jpeg output
Every gpt-image model returns a hosted URL instead of base64

questions.explanation: Generation supports quality levels, transparent backgrounds with png on gpt-image-1, and jpeg output compression. The gpt-image family returns base64 data, not a hosted URL.

questions.stem: An application must ask a multimodal model open-ended questions about a photo and request high visual fidelity. How is the image passed?

options.text: As a separate Image Analysis REST call before the chat
As an image_url content block in a Chat Completions message, optionally with detail set to high
As a base64 field on the SpeechConfig object
As a query parameter on the deployment URL

questions.explanation: Vision-capable chat models accept images as image_url content blocks within a Chat Completions message, and the detail parameter can be set to high for finer analysis at higher token cost.

questions.stem: A cataloging solution needs a bounding box plus a short description for each distinct region of an image. Which Image Analysis feature fits?

options.text: Caption, which returns one sentence for the whole image
Dense Captions, which returns descriptions with bounding boxes for multiple regions
Tags, which returns labels without coordinates
Read OCR, which extracts text lines

questions.explanation: Dense Captions produces a description and bounding box for several regions of an image. Caption returns a single sentence for the entire image, and Tags returns labels without location data.

questions.stem: Because the Chat Completions vision endpoint is stateless, a multi-turn visual Q&A app must resend the relevant image and prior turns with each new question.
questions.explanation: The endpoint does not retain conversation state between calls, so the application must include the image and any needed prior context on every request to preserve continuity.

questions.stem: A web team must generate accessible alt text for uploaded photos to satisfy WCAG guidance. Which Image Analysis capability is the direct fit?

options.text: Read OCR
The Caption feature, which returns a concise human-readable description
Smart crop
Background removal

questions.explanation: The Caption feature returns a short natural-language description suitable for alt text, supporting accessibility requirements such as WCAG 1.1.1. OCR, cropping, and background removal do not describe overall image content.

questions.stem: For Fabrikam Insurance, the team needs a reusable, schema-driven analyzer that extracts fields from embedded photos and reports a confidence value for each field. Which service should they use?

options.text: The Chat Completions vision endpoint with a free-form prompt
Azure AI Content Understanding with a custom analyzer
The Image Analysis Tags feature
The Speech SDK

questions.explanation: Content Understanding lets you define a reusable analyzer with a field schema and can emit per-field confidence and source estimation, which suits repeatable extraction across many similar images. The free-form chat endpoint is not schema-driven or reusable in the same way.

questions.stem: A team uses the Content Understanding prebuilt video analyzer and needs per-shot timing details in the output. What must they set?

options.text: disableFaceBlurring to false
returnDetails to true so cameraShotTimesMs is included
enableSegment to false
The output format to WEBVTT only

questions.explanation: Setting returnDetails to true adds granular metadata such as camera shot times to the analyzer output. WEBVTT is the transcript format and does not by itself include shot timing details.

questions.stem: Which statements correctly distinguish Azure AI Image Analysis from Content Understanding for visual workloads? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Image Analysis offers prebuilt features such as Caption, Dense Captions, Tags, and OCR
Content Understanding lets you define custom field schemas for extraction
Content Understanding can analyze video and return timestamped segments
Image Analysis is the right choice for schema-driven structured field extraction

questions.explanation: Image Analysis provides fixed prebuilt visual features, while Content Understanding supports custom field schemas and multimodal inputs including video with timestamped segments. Schema-driven structured extraction is a Content Understanding strength, not Image Analysis.

questions.stem: A generative image application must defend against prompt-injection instructions hidden inside user-supplied text. When should Prompt Shields be invoked?

options.text: After the model produces its output, to redact the response
Before the content reaches the model, to detect injection attempts
Only during batch processing
Only on generated images, not text

questions.explanation: Prompt Shields analyze user prompts and any embedded document content for jailbreak and injection attacks, and must run before the content is sent to the model so that a detected attack can be blocked.

questions.stem: Which statements about Content Credentials on AI-generated images are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: They are based on the C2PA provenance standard
They are automatically attached to generated images
Removing or altering the image can strip the credential
They are a visible watermark burned into the pixels

questions.explanation: Content Credentials implement the C2PA provenance manifest, are attached automatically to generated images, and can be lost if the image is tampered with. They are cryptographic metadata rather than a visible watermark.
